growth_records.medium: defined P-rich/P-limited minimal medium (Tanner 2007) with glucose as sole organic C
growth_records.cite: Scott, J Thad et al. Variable Stoichiometry and Homeostatic Regulation of Bacterial Biomass Elemental Composition. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00042. [PMC3283892]
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat dilution rates were set at 25% mu_max for each isolate... Aeromonas sp. | Dilution rate (h-1) 0.05 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state => mu = D = 0.05 h-1 (25% mu_max => mu_max ~0.20 h-1). doubling_time = 0.693/0.05 = 13.86 h. No uptake/secretion rates measured (only biomass C, P and C:P_B). Medium recipe cited to Tanner (2007); glucose 27 mM C constant C source. pH not reported. TER(C:P)=155; H' 1.47 (P-sufficient) / 26.3 (P-deficient, strong homeostasis). NOTE: Table 1 also lists Vibrio splendidus (D=0.02), Pseudomonas fluorescens (D=0.03-0.09) and Escherichia coli (D=0.5-1.5) but these are cited FROM other studies (Lovdal et al. 2007; Chrzanowski and Kyle 1996; Makino et al. 2003), NOT experiments in this paper, so no records were created for them.
